hook_text_english: How to set up Claude Cowork in 20 min
full_text_english: How to set up Claude Cowork in 20 min:(even if you've never touched a prompt file)→ 0-5 min: Download the desktop app. Get the paid plan. Create one folder: "Claude Cowork." Inside it, 3 subfolders: ABOUT ME, OUTPUTS, TEMPLATES. → 5-8 min: Build your 3 core files in ABOUT ME/.To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email. → 8-10 min: Paste your Global Instructions. Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions. Instructions: "Read my ABOUT ME/ before every task. Never touch OUTPUTS/ or TEMPLATES/ unless I say so."→ 10-12 min: Install Wispr .ai. Hold a key, talk, release. You type at 60 words per minute. You speak at 150. The bottleneck in Cowork is you.→ 12-20 min: Run your first real task. Speak: "Read my folder and help me write [something you actually need this week]." Answer the questions by talking. Review the output. Pro tip: Keep your files under 6,000 tokens total. If they're bloated, Cowork summarizes them loosely instead of reading them carefully.——To download all of my Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

full_text_english: Stop using ChatGPT for everything.These are the only 7 tools I (actually) swear by:1. Grok.com → for searchChatGPT doesn't know what happened 20 mins agoGrok does. Real-time tweets pulled straight from X.Ask a question. Get facts. Not guesses and links.2. Gemini (google.com) → for images & multilingualGenerates text inside images flawlessly. In any language. Clean. Believable. Editable. Hindi, Spanish, anything. No more 5th try. 3. Gamma.app → for slidesYou can't upload your brand kit to ChatGPT.Gamma actually builds the deck in your branding. The best PowerPoint killer. Period.4. Wispr.ai → for voice-to-textYou type your prompts into ChatGPT like it's 2024.I just talk. Wispr types. And it learns my edits.80 WPM → 200+. Never going back.5. Ask.dig.ai → for TikTok & social trendsChatGPT guesses what's trending. From stale data.Dig scans 100+ real TikToks. Shows what's trendingReal videos & reaction. Zero hallucination. Just ask.6. Granola.ai → for meeting notesYou paste transcripts into ChatGPT after the call.Granola takes notes during the call. Silently.Does NOT join like a bot. Just works.7. Claude.ai → for serious tasksYou rewrite ChatGPT outputs 5x to sound human.Claude gets it right on the first try.Teach it with your voice. Upload .md files. Done.ChatGPT made you work in 2025.These 7 tools work for you in 2026.Save this. Try one tool this week. hook_text_english: How to set up Claude so it never forgets you: Prompts → Projects → Skills
full_text_english: How to set up Claude so it never forgets you:Prompts → Projects → Skills (explained in 3 mins)Prompts = telling a stranger your job every morning.Projects = giving a new hire a binder on day one.Skills = training an employee once. For forever.Step 1: Start with a Prompt (but don't stay there)✦ Open Claude. Type your task. Get an answer.✦ It works. But tomorrow? Claude forgot everything.✦ You re-explain. Again. Every. Single. Chat.✦ That's Level 1. Most people never leave it.Step 2: Move to a Project✦ Go to Claude .ai → Create a Project.✦ Upload your voice file. Upload your instructions.✦ Now every chat inside that Project knows you.✦ Your context, style, and tone stick.But you still have to open the right Project.You still have to say "read my file first."Step 3: Graduate to Skills✦ Open Claude Cowork. ✦ Select Opus 4.7 + Extended Thinking.✦ Prompt: "Use the skill-creator to help me build a skill for [your most repeated task]."Claude interviews you. Answer extensively."I write reports" is useless."I write weekly reports that start with the headline metric, 3 sections max, next steps as bullets" is a Skill.The specificity is the skill.Step 4: Install and test✦ Save the Skill folder.✦ Go to Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Upload.✦ Open a new chat. Type your task normally.✦ The Skill fires on its own. No slash command. ✦ Claude just knows.I just wrote my full Claude Skills breakdown. It covers setup, the skill-creator walkthrough, and the 7 hacks I found buried in Anthropic's docs.Read it here: https://lnkd.in/dq6xHS4pTo download all of my Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.♻️ Repost this to help someone on your team stop re-explaining themselves to Claude every morning.

hook_text_english: How to make viral cheatsheets with AI in 5 mins
full_text_english: How to make viral cheatsheets with AI in 5 mins: (even if you have zero design skills)1. Find a stupidly viral YouTube video in your niche.2. Get the transcript. Copy it.3. Open Claude. Click Customize → Connectors.4. Search "Gamma." Connect your account.5. Now paste this prompt into Claude:"Act like an expert Content Strategist and Information Designer. Your goal is to turn this source into a structured infographic brief, then generate it in Gamma.Here's my source: [PASTE TRANSCRIPT]Part 1: The ExtractionDon't summarize. Pull out the meat:→ Core Thesis: The single most important takeaway, one sentence.→ Key Data/Facts: Specific numbers, case studies, hard facts.→ Golden Quotes: 3–5 verbatim lines that hit hard.→ The Framework: If there's a step-by-step process or model, outline it.Part 2: The Infographic StructureOne focused story, top to bottom. No vague titles like 'Summary of [Topic].'→ A catchy headline→ A one-sentence subtitle (who it's for or why it matters)→ 4–6 clearly labeled sections with a short heading + 1–2 data points each→ A closing takeaway or CTANow send this to Gamma as a Graphic infographic. Use minimal text."6. Claude extracts, structures & sends it to Gamma.7. No copy-pasting between tabs.8. Click the Gamma link Claude gives you.9. Pick your illustration style. Edit it. Done.You just turned a 20-minute video into an infographic you can edit, with AI images.Do it for the videos you've been 'saving for later'.
body_summary_english: Quick tutorial on converting YouTube transcripts into infographic-style cheatsheets using Claude with Gamma connector for zero-design-skill creators.

hook_text_english: 26 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts
full_text_english: 26 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.——/CLEAR clears conversation history and resets context./COST shows token usage for the current session./COMPACT [instructions] compresses the conversation and lets you specify what to keep./RESUME [session] reopens a previous conversation by ID or name./BRANCH [name] creates a new branch from the current conversation./REWIND rolls the conversation and/or code back to an earlier point./RENAME [name] renames the current session. If no name is given, one is generated./EXPORT [filename] exports the conversation as plain text./MODEL [model] switches to another model, such as Sonnet, Opus, or Haiku./USAGE shows plan usage limits and current rate-limit status./EXTRA-USAGE enables extra usage when standard limits are reached./INIT initializes the project with a CLAUDE .md guide./MEMORY edits CLAUDE .md memory files and controls auto-memory./ADD-DIR <path> adds a working directory for file access during the session./DIFF opens a diff viewer for uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs./SECURITY-REVIEW checks pending changes for security issues./PLAN [description] enters plan mode, optionally starting with a task./PERMISSIONS manages allow, ask, and deny rules for tool access./AGENTS manages agent and sub-agent configurations./SKILLS lists all available skills, built-in and custom./PLUGIN manages Claude Code plugins./RELOAD-PLUGINS reloads active plugins without restarting./MCP manages MCP server connections and OAuth authentication./CONFIG opens settings for theme, model, output style, and preferences./THEME changes the color theme, including light, dark, and accessible options./COLOR [color] sets the prompt bar color for the current session.——After months of testing Claude, I built a single prompt library with every prompt I personally use.To access it, complete these 4 steps:1. Subscribe (for free) → how-to-ai.guide.2. Open my welcome email.3. Hit the automatic reply button inside.4. Receive your prompt library + bonus video.

full_text_english: You're still doing these 5 tasks manually. Claude Computer does them (without you):1. Finding freelancers (post, messages, follow-ups).2. Monitoring competitor pricing every Monday.3. Organizing your desktop & downloads.4. Pulling viral ads from Meta Ads into Sheets.5. Repurposing best YouTube videos into short-formHere's how to set it up in 10 minutes:1. Download the Claude desktop app.2. Upgrade to Pro ($20) or Max ($100).3. Go to Settings → Desktop app.4. Turn on Browser use → Turn on Computer use.5. Deny apps you don't want Claude to touch.6. Click on Dispatch in the left sidebar.7. Scan the QR code with your phone's Claude app.8. Now go to your phone. Open Dispatch. 9. Paste these 5 prompt:Prompt 1: Find a freelancer for me"Go to my Fiverr account on Chrome. Post a job offer for [task]. Then, send a message to the best 10 [job needed] for [task]. Check if I have any unanswered DMs, and follow up until one accepts the [task] for [budget] maximum."Prompt 2: Find viral ads from today."Go to the Meta Ads Library: search the latest viral ads about [topic] in [country] only. Create a Google Sheets with at least 50 links to 50 ads that mention [topic].""Now go to Gemini and recreate the ad. But make it about subscribing to my newsletter "How to AI". Each time you generate it on Gemini, I want you to save them on my Canva account in a new folder "Meta Ads". "Prompt 3: Monitor my competitor's pricing."Go to [competitor 1], [competitor 2], and [competitor 3] on Chrome. For each, find the pricing page and extract all plan names, prices, and key features. Compare them side by side in a Google Sheets. Highlight where my pricing at [your website] is higher or lower. Repeat every Monday and flag changes."Prompt 4: Desktop file organization"Go to my Desktop and Downloads folders. Scan every file and sort them into folders: Screenshots, Receipts, PDFs, Images, Documents, Videos, and Other. Rename each file [YYYY-MM-DD]_[short description].[ext]. For receipts, extract vendor, amount & date into a Google Sheets "Receipt Tracker". Delete duplicates. Give me a before/after summary."Prompt 5: Repurpose my best content."Go to my YouTube channel on Chrome. Find my 10 most viewed videos from the past 6 months. For each, open the transcript and extract the 3 most quotable moments (under 30s each). Create a Google Sheets with video title, timestamp, quote text, and a suggested hook for short-form clips. Sort by view count."10. Go outside. Claude texts you updates.11. To Schedule it: Left sidebar → Scheduled → New task → frequency.This is a summary of my newsletter "Claude is now YOUR computer", a free guide to access:↳ https://lnkd.in/dv5wVH3S.To level up, I made a free library of resources.To access it, complete these 4 steps:1. Subscribe (for free) → how-to-ai.guide.2. Open my welcome email.3. Hit the automatic reply button inside.4. Receive your prompt library + bonus video.
body_summary_english: Lists 5 repetitive business tasks that Claude's computer use feature automates, with a 10-minute setup guide to enable the feature via Claude Pro or Max subscription.
comments: The fact that this video feels very old school, with the grainy effect and the colouring grading. It feels unreal that we can go back to the era with the same vibe and create such videos with banger tools.,that's why i couldn't stop watching it, Anisha Jain it felt too real. and the crazy thing is all of it is now prompt-ablebut getting all of them right in a single prompt is a real technical achievement,We’re not entering the AI era.We’re already late to it.The real edge now isn’t access to tools—it’s how fast you learn to use them creatively.Execution is becoming the new intelligence.,Mr Azmaik Sage comment.,Ruben HassidWhat stands out to me isn’t just the quality, but how accessible this is becoming  🤯  The pace of AI video right now is crazy,quality plus free changes who gets to create, Dmytro Klimchuk have you tried this tool before or a similar one?,This is wild! The tech behind LTX 2.3 is a total game, changer. But it also raises questions about originality and authenticity in content. Are we ready for AI, generated shows to compete with human creativity?,it's already happening, Anirudh Sharma the tools exist, the output is convincing, and the cost is zerothe shift is whether human creativity rises to meet the moment or gets lazy because the tools make laziness easy,It’s not just about visuals anymore. It’s about full scene creation in one flow.→ Turns simple prompts into full video scenes→ Generates synced audio without extra tools→ Runs locally, so no heavy cost or wait timeDo you think this will replace traditional video production or just support it?,glad it resonates, Samina Yasmin :)try it when you can,Ruben Hassid The interesting bit is not whether people can tell. It is that creative leverage is moving from studios to laptops, which gets very commercial very fast.,Ruben Hassid well said.,The rapid advancements in AI video generation are truly transformative. Tools like LTX 2.3 democratize access to high-quality content creation, shifting the landscape for professionals. This underscores the critical need for mastering AI skills to remain competitive and visible.,glad it resonates, LaVonne James - Career Coach could you tell it was AI?,Crazy how fast this is moving, the real shift is from tools to storytelling skills now. Ruben Hassid,this does level the playing field for everyone technically, Mandeep Sai the only remaining edge: what story do you want to tell?,I didn't watch a single scene from Seinfeld, and I thought this was real.It's unbelievably fast how AI can exactly replicate a scene from years ago.,all of it from a text prompt, Maria Zhanette Yap a prompt that generated a video convincing enough to pass as authentic to you :),The gap between ‘AI-generated’ and ‘broadcast quality’ just collapsed. What used to need a studio budget now runs on a laptop.,the cost model collapsed too, Karolis Balsiukevicius studio budgets you pay for equipment and time,LTX 2.3 is a massive win for the local-first movement. While Sora stays behind a $1M/day paywall, 4K 50FPS video with native audio is now running on a laptop. This isn't just a "Sora alternative"—it’s the democratization of Netflix-grade production for everyone.,The realism is one thing, but the real shift is accessibility. When this runs locally, the barrier to high-end video creation changes completely.,The comparison between LTX 2.3 and Sora is illuminating. It highlights a practical path for founders to deploy advanced AI without the exorbitant infrastructure costs, especially the emphasis on local execution for tasks that don't require massive scale.,Impressive leap. Evaluate consistency across scenes and editing control before replacing traditional production workflows.,thanks for supporting, Krishna Amarneni could you tell? :),The open source angle is what gets me.. running this locally changes everything about who can actually use it.,The fact that this runs locally on a laptop while Sora burns a million dollars a day to exist is the kind of plot twist that should make every big AI lab nervous.,In 6 months, 'made by an AI' will stop being a disclaimer and start being a credential.,Sooner than people think, maybe 2 years. LTX 2.3 running on a laptop with Netflix-quality output is proof the tools are ready. It's just the mindset that needs to catch up.,It’s getting scary good. At this point, you’re not watching for is this AI? you’re watching for tiny imperfections to catch it.,the search is getting harder every release, Kemisola Ogunsoto did you catch any imperfections?,The quality of AI-generated videos is insane. But I can clearly see that it is AI-generated. No smooth animation transitions when he is moving his head and opening his mouth. Until AI-generated videos can make it pretty smooth and in 4K quality, we are safe.,Crazy to see. The real shift isn’t quality anymore, it’s how fast this becomes accessible to everyone.🔥,Open source video tech is moving incredibly fast. It is getting harder and harder to spot the difference between real footage and AI generations. Ruben,It’s getting really hard to tell if videos are AI or real. And now anyone can create them, not just big companies.,This is wild—every time you think AI has peaked, it drops yet another jaw-dropping demo. That Higgsfield video really does make it look like the uncanny valley is on its final legs! At this rate, soon we’ll need an AI just to keep up with how realistic these AI conversations are getting.If anyone's looking to power this kind of lifelike, engaging interaction on their own site, https://www.chat-data.com/ is right up there with the heavyweights. It lets you craft custom AI representatives with real-time voice chat and even lets your chatbot respond with the nuance and context of a real conversation—AI that doesn’t just talk the talk but walks the virtual walk.,Hard to tell the difference! Just waiting that we see Netflix asking us to prompt to get the video we want.,Its good but eyes are stagnant, no movements, no emotions. Still this is amazing.,Ruben Hassid agree,It’s insane how fast AI goes. We’ll have to check if performance as well as we desire in terms of video 🤍,Ruben 100%. It’s getting harder to spot, but lip sync is still one of the weaknesses, and some movements look less natural. 🤍🤍,These AI tools are getting so good at generating images and videos, i wonder if people can commit robberies or something 😭Ruben,the same argument was always made in every tech, Shantanu Sharma how do you tell real from AI? :),The barrier to entry for video ads just dropped again. Significantly.,the equipment required is now down to a laptop, Michael Collas-Smith (GAICD) how do you spot real from AI? :),The line between real and AI content is getting harder to spot every day.,and the tools doing it now run for free, Eno-obong Akpan how do you spot real vs AI?,people are creating crazy AI videos now man,the volume is exploding, Shushant Lakhyani more especially with tools like LTX getting it this good,This is getting scary good… feels real until you remember it’s not 😅

hook_text_english: Prompting is the worst way to use Claude
full_text_english: Prompting is the worst way to use Claude.Here's what the top 1% do instead:They set up these 8 files once. Then they barely prompt again.File 1: about-me .md (Your identity)Who you are, your job, your priorities. Claude reads this before every task.To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome emailFile 2: voice-profile .md (Your taste DNA)Your beliefs, your writing mechanics, your hard nos. Built from a 100-question interview with Claude.File 3: anti-ai-writing-style .md (Your boundaries)Every word you ban, the structure you reject, tone you hate. 80% of this file is what you're NOT.Go to how-to-ai.guide to download anti AI guide.Don't pay .Open the email. Click on Notion. Open '.md files' Download 'ANTI AI STYLE .md'. File 4: The Cowork Folder (Your 4-folder system)ABOUT ME. PROJECTS. TEMPLATES. CLAUDE OUTPUTS. 3 read-only, 1 write. Nothing extra.File 5: Global Instructions (Your persistent rules)Set once in Settings → Cowork → EditClaude follows them before every task. Prompt: "Always read my files first, never edit my originals, deliver everything to CLAUDE OUTPUTS." File 6: The One Prompt (How you start every chat)29 words. Forces Claude to ask YOU questions. Starts 80% of your conversations.Prompt: "I want to [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. Use AskUserQuestion before you start." File 7: Connectors (Claude inside your tools)Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Gmail, Figma. No copy-pasting. Claude reads your actual tools.File 8: Plugins (Instant skill packs)Marketing, Sales, Legal, Data. One-click install. Each comes with its own slash commands.The secret was always these 8 files behind it.I wrote 2 guides so you can copy my exact system:✦ My full 8-file setup: how-to-ai.guide✦ My Cowork folder walkthrough: claude-co.work(save this to never write a long prompt to Claude)
body_summary_english: Reveals that top 1% users avoid heavy prompting by setting up persistent files (about-me, voice-profile, anti-ai guidelines) once for reuse across all tasks.

hook_text_english: How to use Claude Cowork 4x faster
full_text_english: How to use Claude Cowork 4x faster:Step 1. Stop typing. (I'm serious.) Step 2. Go to wispr .ai.Step 3. Download Wispr Flow. It's free.Step 4. Pick your key shortcut. I use Shift.Step 5. Open Claude Cowork. Hold Shift. Talk.Step 6. Release. Your words appear in the chat box. Step 7. Use this free set up guide: claude-co.workNo integration. No config. No heavy setup. You hold a key, you talk, and it types.✦ Here's why your Cowork sessions are slow:"Let me think about how to word this.""Hold on, I need to type out the context.""I'll just click the default option, close enough.""Let me re-read what I typed before sending."These are killing you. Every session. Every question.Cowork asks you 8 questions. You type each answer in 2 mins. That's 16 mins of you being slow.Spoken? 8 questions. 90 secs. Richer answers.If you type, you self-edit. You delete & rephrase. You kill context before Claude ever sees it.When you talk, you dump your thinking. Raw. Full details that make Cowork's output good.The fix is counterintuitive:→ Stop trying to type better prompts.☑ Talk them. Messy, unfiltered, long. ☑ Cowork figures out what matters.Typed feedback: "Make it less formal."Spoken feedback: "The tone is too stiff. I want it to sound like I'm texting a friend who runs a 200-person company. Keep the data but make it casual. Only redo section 2."Your voice is the context. Stop leaving it out.♻️ Repost to help one person to use Claude faster.
body_summary_english: Free tutorial on using Wispr Flow voice input with Claude for hands-free, faster workflow without complex setup or integrations.

hook_text_english: How to climb all 4 layers of Claude in one weekend
full_text_english: How to climb all 4 layers of Claude in one weekend:(even if you just signed up yesterday)✦ Level 1: Claude Chat (Saturday morning)Go to claude .com/download. Install the app.Pay the $20. Select Opus 4.6 + Extended Thinking. Connect Slack, Drive, Notion through Connectors.Stop writing long prompts. Prompt this instead:"I want to [TASK] for [SUCCESS CRITERIA]. Use AskUserQuestion before you start."Most people stay here forever.You're leaving 90% of Claude untouched.Claude Basics: how-to-claude.ai.——✦ Level 2: Claude Cowork (Saturday afternoon)Go to 'Cowork'. Create a folder "Claude-Cowork."4 subfolders. About me, template, project & outputsCreate about-me .md: what you do, how you do.Create anti-ai-style .md: words you'd never say. Set Global Instructions (Settings → Cowork → Edit): "Always read my files first, never edit my originals, deliver everything to CLAUDE OUTPUTS."You just killed prompting.From now on, your prompt is 2 lines + your folder.Move on to Cowork: claude-co.work——✦ Level 3: Skills + Plugins (Sunday morning)Open Cowork. Type: "Use the skill-creator to help me build a skill for [your most repeated task]."Claude interviews you. Answer and be specific.It generates a SKILL .md. Test it: "When would you use this skill?" If the description is vague, fix it.Upload: Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Upload. Now it fires automatically. No slash command.Install Plugins: Cowork > Customize > Browse pluginSkills with about-me .md. Skill handles the process. Voice file handles tone. Two layers simultaneously.Set up Claude Skills: https://lnkd.in/g9MQUC-Z——✦ Level 4: Code + Computer (Sunday afternoon)Click the Code tab. Create a folder. Connect GitHub (free account → Settings → Connectors).Prompt: "Create a GitHub repo named [project]. Code everything. Don't ask for permissions."Download VS Code. Install the Claude extension. Turn on "Skip Permissions" to go 100x faster.After your session, paste this: "Create a CLAUDE .md file with everything you learned about this project." Now Claude remembers your fonts, colors, and structure forever.Claude code guide: https://lnkd.in/gsRhsFxTClaude Computer: Settings → Desktop app → turn on Browser use + Computer use. Connect your phone with Dispatch. Text a task. Schedule a recurring task: left sidebar → Scheduled → write the prompt → pick the frequency.Claude Computer: https://lnkd.in/dSaSqRxwThis is where you stop working inside Claude.Claude starts working on your computer.——Saturday, you were prompting like it's ChatGPT.Sunday, Claude is running your screen, building your website, & sending you text updates.That's all 4 layers. In one weekend.

hook_text_english: How to understand every Claude feature in 5 mins:
full_text_english: How to understand every Claude feature in 5 mins: (even if you've only ever used the basic chat)Chat = texting a stranger who forgets you.Features = hiring a team that already knows the job.Full Setup = training Claude that runs without you.Layer 1: The basics you already know✦ Chat: Open Claude. You type. You get an answer.✦ Extended Thinking: It reasons deeper.✦ Opus 4.6: The smartest model. Always select it.✦ That's where 95% of people stop.✦ Claude 101: how-to-claude.ai.Layer 2: The features that remember you✦ Projects: Upload file & instructions. It remembers.✦ Scoped Memory. Prompt "do like & it knows it.✦ Context Files: Drop everything you know into files✦ about-me .md: A file with what you do & think.✦ Download my files at how-to-guide.ai (for free).✦ Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email.Layer 3: The features that work for you✦ Skills: Long instructions living inside the AI. ✦ Skill Creator: It interviews you to build the Skills.✦ SKILL .md: Trigger file, description & instructions. ✦ Negative Triggers: The "Do NOT use for..." lines. ✦ Claude Skills 101: https://lnkd.in/dq6xHS4p Layer 4: The features that replace your workflow✦ Cowork: It reads, creates, and executes.✦ Cowork Projects: Memory that persists.✦ Global Instructions: Instructions across all chats. ✦ Scheduled Tasks: Claude runs tasks automatically✦ Dispatch: Control Claude from your phone.✦ Claude Cowork 101: claude-co.workLayer 5: The features that connect everything✦ Connectors: Plug into Slack, Calendar & Gmail. ✦ Plugins: Build and upload your own app.✦ Claude Computer: Clicks, browse & operates Mac✦ Claude Code: English is the new code to ship fast✦ AskUserQuestion: Interactive form to prompt you.✦ Gamma: Claude for brief. Gamma for slides.✦ CLAUDE .md: A memory file for Claude Code. ✦ Claude Code 101: https://lnkd.in/d4qamGtMThat's all 25 features. On one page.I turned this into a full visual infographic you can save, print, or send to your team.To download all of my Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.♻️ Repost this to help someone use Claude in 2026.

hook_text_english: How to set up Claude Cowork completely in 1 hour:
full_text_english: How to set up Claude Cowork completely in 1 hour:(duplicate my exact folder, files, and prompts)1. Download the Claude desktop app.2. Click the Cowork tab at the top.3. You need a Pro plan ($20/mo). Worth it.-----→ 0-10 min: Build your 'Claude Cowork' folder.Create "CLAUDE COWORK" on your computer.Inside: About me, Projects, Templates, Output files.To download mine, go here: how-to-ai.guide.Don't pay anything. It's free in the welcome email(The most important step, don't skip it)→ 10-25 min: Write your .md files.about-me .md = who you are, how you work.anti-ai-style .md = every word you'd never use.To download my files, go to how-to-ai.guide.(These two files replace 500-word prompts forever)→ 25-35 min: Set Global Instructions.Go to Settings → Cowork → Edit Global Instructions.Folder rules. Naming conventions.What Claude must read before every task.(You write this once. It runs every time)→ 35-45 min: Create your first Cowork Project.Cowork tab → Projects → +.Pick a task you do every week.Scoped memory = it remember what it did last week(You stop re-explaining yourself)→ 45-55 min: Run your first real task.Prompt: "I want to [task]. Ask me questions first."Claude generates clickable forms to prompt you.It creates a real .docx in your folder.(Stop prompting. Start directing)→ 55-60 min: Schedule a task."Every Monday at 7am, create my weekly briefing."You wake up to a finished doc. That's the endgame.Pro tip: Always add "Use AskUserQuestion" to every prompt. Claude prompts you instead.To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

hook_text_english: Your writing still sounds like AI-written text. Here's a secret hack to humanize it:
full_text_english: Your writing still sounds like AI-written text. Here's a secret hack to humanize it:1. Go to how-to-ai.guide to download anti AI guide.2. Then enter your work email.3. It will ask you to pay or not. Don't pay.4. Wait 2 min. Then open the welcoming email.5. Click on my Notion. Open '.md files'5. Download 'ANTI AI WRITING STYLE .md'. 6. Go to Claude .ai. Don't type your prompt yet.7. Click the '+' button. Upload the anti AI .md first.8. Start every chat with this prompt (copy & paste):"Read my anti-AI writing style file first. It contains every known pattern of AI writing I want to avoid. Apply these as rules to everything you write for me. Do NOT start writing yet - ask me clarifying questions first."By the way, that's how you can tell this is AI-written:1. Stop making it meaningful. 2. The word "is" still exists. Stop writing "serves as."3. Banned words: "underscore, foster, enhance."4. Stop stapling "-ing" phrases to facts. That's fake.5. "Not just X, but also Y" answers nothing.6. Spot the "despite" loop. "Despite success." 7. Name a source, not a list of publications.8. Use a name twice. Not "the artist or figure."9. Calm down with em dashes. Commas still work.10. Check for Markdown leftovers. Asterisks and ##.11. Read it out loud. AI tone means one prompt.12. Details should do something. People still can't tell when AI wrote something.You want to help your network spot it?♻️ Repost this list so they stop falling for it.

Start here at how-to-claude.aiStep 4. There is no step 5.But here's the mistake:Don't start a new chat every time.Keep going in the same one.Claude gets smarter the more context it has.✦ Claude ProjectsStep 1. Click "Projects" in the sidebar.Step 2. Create a new project.Step 3. Upload your brand voice, docs & references.Step 4. Download my files at how-to-ai.guideStep 5. Don't pay anything, get it in welcome email.It's like handing a new hire a binder on 1st day."Here's how we do things."Every chat after that is informed by that binder.✦ Claude CoworkStep 1. Download the Claude desktop app.Step 2. Click "Cowork" next to Chat.Step 3. Go to this free guide: claude-co.workStep 4. Grant access to your folders.Step 5. Describe the outcome you want.This is the one that replaces your intern.It opens your files. Does the work. Hands you the deliverable. You just review it.✦ Claude SkillsStep 1. Create a SKILL .md file.Step 2. Write out how to do one task perfectly.Step 3. Upload it in Settings → Skills.Step 4. Use Claude Skills: https://lnkd.in/dq6xHS4pStep 5. Claude detects when it's relevant.You don't teach a chef what "cooking" is.You hand them a specific recipe.Skills are recipes.✦ Claude CodeStep 1. Download the Claude desktop app.Step 2. Click the "Code" tab at the top.Step 3. Create a folder on your computer.Step 4. Claude Code: https://lnkd.in/d4qamGtMStep 5. Connect GitHub.You now have a junior developer on call 24/7.You describe the vision. Claude builds it.Pro tip: The biggest mistake across all 5:Describing steps instead of outcomes.Don't say "Open file A, copy column B."Say "Combine the revenue data into one summary."Let Claude figure out the how.To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Build your "Claude Cowork" folder✦ One folder on your desktop. Three subfolders.✦ /voice - your tone file and anti-AI writing guide.✦ /context - your offer, audience & examples.✦ /templates - your best past work. The standard.✦ My full folder structure: how-to-ai.guideSet-up 3. Set up Projects before you ever prompt✦ One Project per recurring task. Not per topic.✦ Upload your voice file + 2-3 examples of output.✦ Write custom instructions thoroughly.✦ My Project setup: https://lnkd.in/d8BHqTvVSet-up 4. Stop writing prompts. Start interviewing.✦ Copy-paste my interview prompt✦ From this guide: https://lnkd.in/eF56s4i8✦ It asks you 100 questions about how you think.✦ You answer. The draft writes itself.Set-up 5. Use Claude Dispatch from your phone✦ Text Claude a task. Walk away. Go live your life.✦ It opens Chrome. Posts and messages for you.✦ You come back to updates, not to-do lists.✦ My Dispatch setup: https://lnkd.in/dSaSqRxwSet-up 6. Let Claude build what you describe✦ Say it in English. Claude writes the code.✦ Dashboards. Websites. Internal tools. No coding.✦ Describe it like you'd explain it to a teammate.✦ Claude code guide: https://lnkd.in/gsRhsFxTSet-up 7. Review like a boss. Not like a user.✦ Read v1. Name every single wrong thing.✦ Push back: "This sounds like AI. hook_text_english: 32 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts (worth saving)
full_text_english: 32 Claude shortcut hacks for faster prompts:(worth saving for later)Add one of these at the very start of your prompt.Example: ELI5: [your topic] → get a simple, kid-friendly explanation.——/ELI5 is used to explain as if to a 5-year-old./TLDL summarizes a very long text in a few lines./STEP-BY-STEP lays out reasoning step by step./CHECKLIST turns a response into a checklist./EXEC SUMMARY gives a quick executive-style summary./ACT AS makes ChatGPT speak in a specific role./BRIEFLY forces a very short answer./JARGON asks to use technical vocabulary./AUDIENCE adapts the response to a chosen audience./TONE changes the tone (formal, funny, dramatic, etc.)./DEV MODE simulates a raw, technical developer style./PM MODE gives a project-management perspective./SWOT produces a strengths/weaknesses/opportunities/threats analysis./FORMAT AS enforces a specific format (table, JSON, etc.)./COMPARE puts two or more things side by side./MULTI-PERSPECTIVE shows several points of view./CONTEXT STACK keeps multiple layers of context in memory./BEGIN WITH / END WITH forces starting or ending with something./ROLE: TASK: FORMAT: explicitly defines the role, the task, and the expected format./SCHEMA generates a structured outline or a data model./REWRITE AS: rephrases in a requested style./REFLECTIVE MODE prompts the AI to reflect on its own answer./SYSTEMATIC BIAS CHECK asks to identify biases./DELIBERATE THINKING forces slower, more thoughtful reasoning./NO AUTOPILOT forbids superficial, autopilot responses./EVAL-SELF asks for a critical self-evaluation of the response./PARALLEL LENSES examines from several angles in parallel./FIRST PRINCIPLES rebuilds from fundamental basics./CHAIN OF THOUGHT shows intermediate reasoning./PITFALLS identifies possible traps and errors./METRICS MODE expresses answers with measures and indicators./GUARDRAIL sets strict boundaries not to cross.——After months of testing Claude, I built a single prompt library with every prompt I personally use.To access it, complete these 4 steps:1. Subscribe (for free) → how-to-ai.guide.2. Open my welcome email.3. Hit the automatic reply button inside.4. Receive your prompt library + bonus video.

full_text_english: The $20/month Claude plan is enough. But only if you stop making these 17 mistakes:1: You upload PDFs raw. One page = 3,000 tokens.Fix: Paste the text into a Google doc. Download as .md format. Under 200 tokens.2: You build files inside Cowork too early.Fix: Plan in Chat first. Move to Cowork only when you know exactly what you want.3: You write 500-word prompts that reload.Fix: Write 29 words instead: "I want to [task] to [goal]. Ask me questions using AskUserQuestion."4: You say "redo the whole thing" to correct part 3.Fix: "Only redo section 3. Keep everything else. No commentary. Just the output."5: You send 3 separate messages for 3 tasks. Fix: One message, three tasks. "Summarize this, list the points, suggest a headline."6: You type "No, I meant," stacking on the history.Fix: Click 'Edit' on your original message. Fix it. Regenerate. History replaced, not added.7: You use the Opus model for a grammar check. Fix: Sonnet or Haiku for quick tasks. Save Opus + Extended Thinking for deep work.8: You dump 50 files into Cowork "just in case."Fix: Only include what this task needs. Zero folders for quick tasks like email drafts.9: You never restart fresh & keep having long chats.Fix: Every 15-20 messages → summarize, copy the brief, start a fresh session.10: You keep 3 topics in 1 chat. Claude re-reads all.Fix: New topic = new chat. Always. Dead context is dead tokens.11: Your about-me file is 22,000 words (too long).Fix: Trim to under 2,000 words. End sessions with "Write a session-notes .md."Paste my .md file prompt: https://lnkd.in/gzzhvrY612: You leave search & connectors on by default.Fix: Default everything off. Turn features on per task, not per account.13: You upload the same PDF to 5 different chats. Fix: Use Projects. Upload once. Every chat inside references it without re-burning tokens.14: You skip Personal Preferences & waste setup.Fix: Settings → Personal Preferences. Set your tone and style once. It persists forever.15: You rewrite prompts from scratch every time.Fix: Keep a prompt library. Same structure, swap the variable. Stable prompts get cached.16: You manually run the same report every week.Fix: Use /schedule. "Every Monday at 7am, create my weekly briefing." Wake up to a finished doc.17: You use Claude for things it can't do.Fix: Know your tools. Images → Gemini. Real-time search → Grok. Stop burning tokens on dead ends.-----To download all of my Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

Number 9 is the one most people learn too late — long chat histories don't just waste tokens, they actively degrade response quality because the model tries to stay "consistent" with earlier context that's already outdated. Starting fresh with a tight brief isn't a workaround, it's just how the tool actually works best. The PDF → markdown tip alone is worth bookmarking though, that compression ratio is wild if you've never tested it.
Long chats make the model actively work against the task, Philipp Krabatsch fresh session with the right context is the fix
Token waste is invisible. That's what makes it dangerous.
you dont feel the cost until the session falls apart, How to Prompt

its another message Claude will keep reading as the chat gets longer, Maria Zhanette Yap editing is the obvious fix

hook_text_english: Stop watching 45-minute Claude tutorials. Everything you need is in one image.
full_text_english: Stop watching 45-minute Claude tutorials. Everything you need is in this one image (save this):1. Cowork Setup (Tips 1–10)☑ Download the desktop app. Not the browser.☑ Create one folder on your PC called 'COWORK'.☑ 4 subfolders: About me, project, template, output☑ Download anti-ai-style here: how-to-ai.guide.☑ Subscribe for free. Open my welcome email.☑ Hit the automatic reply button inside.☑ Download anti-ai & my about me from my Notion.2. Pick the Right Model (Tips 11–20)☑ Opus 4.6 + Extended Thinking for complex tasks.☑ Sonnet = quick edits. Haiku = scanning files.☑ The model matters less than the prompt. ☑ Bad prompt on Opus > Great prompt on Haiku.3. Prompting (Tips 21–30)☑ Stop writing long prompts. Files > prompts.☑ One task per prompt. One. Not five.☑ Say "Does NOT sound like" to kill the AI voice.☑ Give the task, not the method. Let it figure it out.4. AskUserQuestion Tool (Tips 31–40)☑ "Start with AskUserQuestion" in all 1st prompts.☑ Claude builds you a clickable form. Click answers.☑ It asks the right questions so you don't have to.☑ If the direction is wrong, say it. It rebuilds.5. Connectors (Tips 41–50)☑ Settings → Connectors → Browse → Click "Add."☑ Slack, Google Drive, Notion, Gmail. 50+ tools.☑ Claude reads your actual files. No copy-pasting.☑ Free on all plans. No extra cost. Just connect.6. Plugins (Tips 51–60)☑ Cowork → Customize → Browse → Install.☑ Marketing, Legal, Sales, Data — pick your role.☑ Type / to trigger any plugin command instantly.☑ Customize to match your company and voice.7. Claude in Excel (Tips 61–70)☑ Install "Claude by Anthropic" from Microsoft Marketplace.☑ It reads every tab. Explains formulas in English.☑ Drop a PDF in. Claude extracts the tables for you.☑ No macros. Claude highlights what it touches.8. Projects & Teams (Tips 71–80)☑ One Project per deliverable. Not per client.☑ Upload a great example. It matches the standard.☑ Convert one person first. Then scale to the team.☑ Use the 15-minute demo. Show, don't tell.9. Artifacts (Tips 81–90)☑ Charts, dashboards, trackers - inside the chat.☑ They work automatically in Cowork.☑ Preview before you export. Edit it live. Then copy.☑ Share with non-Claude users as HTML. 10. Advanced Mastery (Tips 91–100)☑ Keep your files under 200 lines. Shorter is better.☑ 80% of your file should be what you're NOT.☑ Review outputs. Especially financial work.☑ Claude does 80% busywork. You do the 20%.---To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.♻️ Repost this to save someone 6 months of trial.

hook_text_english: How to set up Claude Skills completely in 1 hour (duplicate my exact setup)
full_text_english: How to set up Claude Skills completely in 1 hour:(duplicate my exact folder, skills, and prompts)1. Open the Claude desktop app.2. Click the Cowork tab at the top.3. You need a Pro plan ($20/mo). Worth it.4. Start with this (free) guide: claude-skills.free-----→ 0-10 min: Open Cowork. Find the skill-creator.Select your folder. Opus 4.6 + Extended thinking.Prompt: "Use the skill-creator to help me build a skill for [your most repeated task]."→ 10-25 min: Answer the interview. Build the Skill.The skill-creator asks you questions. Be specific.Useless: "I write reports."Skill that works: "Weekly reports, headline metric first, 3 sections max, next steps at the end."It generates a folder + SKILL .md with your trigger & instructions. (Be specific or it won't fire)→ 25-35 min: Run the evaluation.Claude creates an eval to validate your Skill.Click "View the eval results" to test before downloading. (Write this once. It runs every time)→ 35-45 min: Install and test.Save the Skill folder. Go to Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Upload.Type a request that should trigger the Skill.Download my skills hacks here: claude-skills.free→ 45-55 min: Iterate and debug.Try 5 phrasings. Does the Skill fire each time?Try 3 unrelated requests. Does it stay quiet?Ask: "When would you use this skill?" It quotes the description back. (You instantly see what's broken)→ 55-60 min: Browse plugins. Download more SkillsDesktop app → Customize → Personal plugins → Browse plugins.A plugin = a bundle of Skills. Download & test.Or go to makemyskill .com. Describe it, download it and upload it.Pro tip: Always add "Do NOT use for…" lines to your description. Negative triggers matter more than positive ones. Skills are portable. Same SKILL .md works on Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT.To download all of my other Claude infographics:Step 1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.Step 2. Subscribe for free. Don't pay anything.Step 3. Open my welcome email (most skip this).Step 4. Hit the automatic reply button inside.Step 5. Download my infographics from my Notion.Bonus. Enjoy my best copy-paste prompts, too.

full_text_english: Claude broke the internet (again) with Claude Design.Here's how it designs websites, decks & videos:1. You need a Pro or Max plan.2. Go to claude .ai/design (separate URL).3. Team/Enterprise? Your admin turns it on via Settings → Capabilities → Anthropic Labs.If you want a free guide with screenshots, go here:↳ claudedesign.freeHere are my favorite use cases:✦ Pitch a $100M fundraise in one shot.Prompt: "Create a high-fidelity landing page designed to raise $[AMOUNT] from [TARGET INVESTORS] for '[PRODUCT NAME]' - [short description]. Target audience: [audience]. Tone should feel [feeling] - think a mix of [brand] + [brand] + [ecosystem]."✦ Turn any blog post into a pitch deck.Prompt 1 (upload the blog as markdown): "Make a 30-second animated video that summarizes this blog for a first-time viewer."Prompt 2 (same chat): "Now convert that video into a slide pitch deck."The video step forces visual thinking first. The deck comes out 10x better than asking for it directly.✦ Design like Mastercard, Airbnb, or Ferrari.Go to getdesign .md (free). Download the DESIGN .md of any brand you love.Upload it to Claude Design + your product brief.Prompt: "Use the attached DESIGN .md. Create a [landing page / pitch deck] for [my product]."It designs in that brand's exact style. Fonts, colors, tone, everything.I made over 100+ infographics like this.If you want to download all of them, just:1. Go to how-to-ai.guide.2. Then enter your work email.3. It will ask you to pay or not. Don't pay.4. Wait 2 min. Then open the welcoming email.5. Click on my Notion library with everything in it.Disclaimer: you will be subscribed to my newsletter. It's free, and always will be. Some people pay only to join my community & get answers faster.500,000 readers enjoy it twice a week.To always stay ahead of the AI curve.To master AI, before it masters you.♻️ Repost this to help others get better at Claude.
